stem: A developer writes most of a project in a high-level language, but uses assembly language (a low-level language) for a tiny, performance-critical section.
model_answer: High-level languages use English-like keywords and work with variables, loops and functions; they are easier to read, write and maintain and are portable between different processors because they are translated for each machine. Low-level languages (machine code / assembly) are close to the processor's instruction set, so they run very fast and give the programmer fine control over hardware, but are hard to read and specific to one processor family.

mark_points.text: high-level languages are closer to English / easier to read and write
high-level languages are portable between different processors
accepted_alternatives: HLLs can run on different hardware with re-translation

mark_points.text: low-level languages are close to machine code / run very quickly
accepted_alternatives: assembly/machine code executes with minimal overhead

mark_points.text: low-level languages give fine control over hardware but are processor-specific and hard to read
accepted_alternatives: low-level code tied to one CPU architecture
